event: The Virgin and Child surrounded by a garland of flowers
symbols: garland of flowers — the beauty and abundance of creation honoring its Creator
roses — Mary's love and the blood of Christ's Passion
white lilies — purity and the virginity of Mary
Virgin holding the Child — the Incarnation, God made flesh through Mary
oval frame of flowers — the paradise garden (hortus conclusus) surrounding the Mother and Son
description: At center, within a painted oval, the Virgin Mary in a red dress and blue mantle holds the infant Jesus, who reaches toward her. They are set against a dark background. Surrounding this central image is a lavish garland of flowers — roses in red, pink, and white, lilies, tulips, irises, and other blossoms — forming a thick oval wreath. The flowers are rendered with meticulous botanical detail against a dark brown background. The garland is abundant and varied, with blooms at every stage from bud to full flower.
theological_description: This collaborative work unites the devotional image of the Madonna and Child with a sumptuous floral garland, a genre that carries deep theological resonance. The flowers surrounding Mary and Jesus evoke the Song of Solomon's imagery of the beloved as a garden enclosed (Song of Solomon 4:12) — the hortus conclusus that became a symbol of Mary's perpetual virginity. The white lilies signify Mary's purity (as in the Annunciation tradition drawn from Luke 1:28, 'full of grace'), while the red roses point to Christ's Passion and Mary's co-suffering. The abundance and variety of flowers celebrate the goodness of creation (Genesis 1:31) now glorifying its Creator held in human arms. The theological heart remains the Incarnation: 'The Word became flesh' (John 1:14), here shown as a tender infant cradled by his mother, surrounded by the beauty of the world he made.

scripture_refs.reason: The Word became flesh — the Incarnation at the center of the composition
Blessed are you among women and blessed is the fruit of your womb — Mary honored with creation's beauty
A garden enclosed is my sister, my bride — the hortus conclusus symbolism of the floral garland
I am a rose of Sharon, a lily of the valleys — the floral imagery associated with the beloved

_meta.title: The Virgin and Child in a Garland of Flowers
